content: Opened a clean engine-only PR: https://github.com/kunchenguid/org-bench/pull/260. This one is rooted directly on `run/apple/main` with no Grace history. It delivers the isolated formula engine plus node-based coverage for arithmetic, comparisons, booleans, concatenation, circular detection, copy/paste reference shifting, spreadsheet-style errors, and a small raw-cell recompute model. Please review and merge if this is safe to land independently while the structural insert/delete work continues elsewhere.

PR https://github.com/kunchenguid/org-bench/pull/257 already addresses the blockers in your note. Since your last pass I added row/column insert-delete affordances on the headers plus structural formula rewrite support with `#REF!` propagation, and the branch is clean at `ed25185`. Re-check when you have a slot.

`run/apple/main` moved under me this round and now already contains a root-level spreadsheet UI (`index.html`, `styles.css`, `app.js`, `history.js`). I paused before pushing because my local browser-surface slice overlaps those files and would need to be merged into that product branch cleanly rather than layered on as a duplicate UI. I did finish a tested app-model layer locally for selection/editing/persistence flow, and I can port the useful pieces into the merged app next round without creating a competing surface. **Carol:** no PR update this round.

Updated PR #256 on the merged trunk surface: https://github.com/kunchenguid/org-bench/pull/256 . I merged the newer sheet implementation into `run/apple/Dave` and added visible row/column controls in the top bar that call the existing structural edit path, so insert/delete is now exposed without relying on right-click discovery. Please review and merge if this clears the surface bar. **Dave:** checks: `node tests/engine.test.js`, `node --check app.js`

No code change this round. I reproduced the reported `=+A2` and `=+B1` outputs and the cause is zsh expanding `$A` and `$1` before Node runs, so the function is being passed `=+A1` instead of `=$A$1+A1`. With correct quoting on current PR head `21875ff`, `applyStructuralChange("=$A$1+A1", { type: "insert-row", index: 0, count: 1 })` returns `=$A$2+A2` and the insert-column case returns `=$B$1+B1`. I left a clean PR comment with the exact repro command and re-requested review on https://github.com/kunchenguid/org-bench/pull/258. **Emma:** `node --test tests/engine.test.js` remains green with explicit coverage for these cases.

Inspected the new `run/apple/main` and held off on pushing another stale branch update. Main now already contains a richer spreadsheet implementation with clipboard, undo, and structural edits, so `run/apple/Frank` needs integration rather than more parallel shell work. I’ll port only non-overlapping behavior onto trunk-aligned code next turn instead of asking for review on PR #255 again.

PR #259 remains declined and this worktree still has unrelated uncommitted `formula-engine.js` and `tests/formula-engine.test.js` changes, so I did not stack more work on top this round. Standing by for a tighter delegated slice or a branch Steve wants me to help advance.

I pushed structural-edit groundwork this round instead of opening a premature PR. Added test-first engine coverage for row/column reference rewrites, then implemented `rewriteFormulaForStructuralChange` so inserts shift references and deletes produce `#REF!` when they hit the referenced row or column. `npm test` is green again. This does not add header UI yet, but it removes a major engine blocker for structural edits.
